tweets.content:
UPDATE: New details have emerged in the case of U.S. national Jordan Brown, who was detained near the India–Nepal border.

According to police, Brown claims to be a former U.S. Navy serviceman who lost his passport in Thailand before travelling through Sri Lanka and arriving in India by sea in November 2025. He also claims to have visited 70 countries and is married to an Indian woman.

An FIR has been registered under the Immigration and Foreigners Act, and authorities are verifying his identity, travel history, and claims.

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

BREAKING: U.S. National Detained Near India–Nepal Border

The Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) has detained a U.S. national near the Sonauli border crossing in Uttar Pradesh after he attempted to enter Nepal illegally, without any valid travel documents.

The individual, who identified himself as Jordan Brown from California, reportedly tried to flee when stopped. Security personnel recovered ₹31,460 in cash and two mobile phones, but no documents confirming his identity.

Brown reportedly claimed to be a former US Navy and Special Forces member, although Indian authorities have not verified those claims. During questioning, he failed to produce a valid passport, visa, or any other travel documents.

The case is under investigation, and authorities have not yet announced any formal charges.

The position was conveyed by MEA Secretary Sripriya Ranganathan at the Palestine Donor Group meeting in Brussels. India also announced new development and humanitarian projects in Palestine, focusing on healthcare, education, rehabilitation, vocational training, and women's empowerment. The statement reaffirms India’s longstanding policy of supporting a negotiated two-state solution alongside continued development assistance to the Palestinian people.
tweets.content:
India Supports Palestine’s UN Membership

India has reiterated its support for Palestine’s bid for full United Nations membership and reaffirmed its longstanding backing for a two-state solution, where Israel and Palestine coexist in peace within internationally recognized borders.

The position was conveyed by MEA Secretary Sripriya Ranganathan at the Palestine Donor Group meeting in Brussels.

India also announced new development and humanitarian projects in Palestine, focusing on healthcare, education, rehabilitation, vocational training, and women's empowerment.

The statement reaffirms India’s longstanding policy of supporting a negotiated two-state solution alongside continued development assistance to the Palestinian people.

tweets.title: Border States Flag Rising Cross-Border Security Threats At the first-ever conference of SPs from 119 border districts, states highlighted growing security concerns along India's land borders. Key issues raised: - Increased drone drops of weapons and narcotics along the Pakistan border. - Use of encrypted apps such as WhatsApp, Skred, and Zangi by cross-border criminal and terror networks. - Cases of illegal migrants obtaining forged identity documents and appearing on electoral rolls. - Concerns over the open Nepal border being exploited by extremist elements. - Migration from border villages due to poor infrastructure, raising long-term security concerns. Home Minister Amit Shah called for closer monitoring of demographic changes in border areas and stronger coordination between border communities, police, and security agencies.
tweets.content:
Border States Flag Rising Cross-Border Security Threats

At the first-ever conference of SPs from 119 border districts, states highlighted growing security concerns along India's land borders.

Key issues raised:

- Increased drone drops of weapons and narcotics along the Pakistan border.
- Use of encrypted apps such as WhatsApp, Skred, and Zangi by cross-border criminal and terror networks.
- Cases of illegal migrants obtaining forged identity documents and appearing on electoral rolls.
- Concerns over the open Nepal border being exploited by extremist elements.
- Migration from border villages due to poor infrastructure, raising long-term security concerns.

Home Minister Amit Shah called for closer monitoring of demographic changes in border areas and stronger coordination between border communities, police, and security agencies.

tweets.title: I heard someone argue that air-to-air missiles are ordered in tranches because they have a finite service life. That’s correct but that is only part of the story and from very user seller point of view. Take the Indian Air Force’s order for roughly 240 Astra missiles. Yes, a missile has a shelf life, and yes, if it is not kept in a temperature-controlled environment its life can reduce further. All of that is fair. But the real issue is: if you place missile orders with a clear long-term roadmap, the manufacturing ecosystem can plan properly. It knows how much to produce, when to deliver, and where to invest in infrastructure. It can build a little extra capacity to handle crisis situations, so you are not forced to look outside for emergency purchases when demand suddenly spikes. That is why a 20-year roadmap for missiles and manufacturing makes far more sense than simply ordering 240 today and then waiting to see when all the MKI are ready to carry them, or when the Mk1A fleet finally arrives. That should not be just procurement. It should be full lifecycle planning with proper phase out replacement etc built it. Instead we are arguing we will order it when MKI are modified or MK1A are delivered. This very thought process has placed IAF where it stands today.

tweets.title: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully completed three critical tests for the Gaganyaan Crew Module, advancing India's first human spaceflight mission. 🔹 Crew Module Uprighting System: Validated the cold-gas system that restores the capsule to an upright position after sea splashdown. 🔹 Umbilical Separation Test: Successfully demonstrated the clean separation of the crew and service modules, a crucial step before atmospheric re-entry. 🔹 Apex Cover Separation: Confirmed the structural integrity of the crew module during jettison of the apex cover, enabling safe parachute deployment.
tweets.content:
The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully completed three critical tests for the Gaganyaan Crew Module, advancing India's first human spaceflight mission.

🔹 Crew Module Uprighting System: Validated the cold-gas system that restores the capsule to an upright position after sea splashdown.
🔹 Umbilical Separation Test: Successfully demonstrated the clean separation of the crew and service modules, a crucial step before atmospheric re-entry.
🔹 Apex Cover Separation: Confirmed the structural integrity of the crew module during jettison of the apex cover, enabling safe parachute deployment.

tweets.title: Pakistan Army has launched yet another military operation in Balochistan. Another Arabic name. Same conflict. Major publicly known operations include: • Operation Arrow (1973 to 1977) • Operation Radd ul Fasaad (2017 onwards) • Operation Radd ul Fitna 1 (2026) • Operation Shaban (2026) Alongside these, hundreds of unnamed clearance, combing, and intelligence based operations have been carried out across Balochistan over the past two decades. Balochistan is vast and unforgiving. It is not anyone's playground where forces can simply walk in, conduct operations, and expect the conflict to disappear. Video credit to the respective owners.
tweets.content:
Pakistan Army has launched yet another military operation in Balochistan. Another Arabic name. Same conflict.

Major publicly known operations include:
• Operation Arrow (1973 to 1977)
• Operation Radd ul Fasaad (2017 onwards)
• Operation Radd ul Fitna 1 (2026)
• Operation Shaban (2026)

Alongside these, hundreds of unnamed clearance, combing, and intelligence based operations have been carried out across Balochistan over the past two decades.

Balochistan is vast and unforgiving. It is not anyone's playground where forces can simply walk in, conduct operations, and expect the conflict to disappear.

Video credit to the respective owners.
